mnemonics.title: MESI States Explained
mnemonics.content: Modified = dirty exclusive (only copy, changed from memory). Exclusive = clean exclusive (only copy, matches memory). Shared = clean shared (multiple copies, all match memory). Invalid = stale (not valid data).

mnemonics.title: Snooping vs Directory
mnemonics.content: Snooping: all caches Spy on the bus (broadcast, simple, limited scalability). Directory: a Dedicated tracker records which caches have each line (point-to-point messages, complex, scales well). Think: Snooping is gossip (everyone hears), Directory is certified mail (targeted).

mnemonics.title: Write-Invalidate vs Write-Update
mnemonics.content: Invalidate: 'Destroy other copies, they will re-fetch if needed.' Less bus traffic for multiple writes before a read. Update: 'Broadcast new value to all copies.' Less latency for reads after a write. Most real systems use write-invalidate.

mnemonics.title: False Sharing: F.A.L.S.E.
mnemonics.content: Fake sharing, Accidental cache-line collision, Latency increases, Same cache line but different data, Eliminated by padding. Remember: the data is NOT truly shared -- it just happens to be on the same cache line.

mnemonics.title: MESI Transition Key Pattern
mnemonics.content: Read alone -> Exclusive. Read by another -> both become Shared. Write by owner (Exclusive) -> Modified (silent, no bus). Write by owner (Shared) -> Modified (invalidate others). Read while Modified -> write back, both become Shared.
